summary: Ran 21 edge-case groups (amount boundaries, ratio boundaries, type/field handling, unicode/length, timestamps, state-dependent). Found 1 critical structural bug: the transaction is INSERT-ed into SQLite BEFORE get_user_average is called, so every first-time transaction includes itself in its own baseline average (self-contamination). This collapses the ratio to 1.0 for all new users, silently defeating the ratio-based detection path. Secondary findings: negative amounts accepted with no validation and rated LOW (ratio goes negative, compares as < 5, falls through to LOW); duplicate transaction IDs cause unhandled 500 (no 409 response); 5000-char location field accepted and stored with no truncation; empty and non-date timestamps accepted silently; already-frozen user accounts continue to be re-frozen on subsequent HIGH transactions (no idempotency guard, extra DB writes).

rows.got: LOW (avg=$4999.99 self-contaminated, ratio=1.0 — amount rule misses by $0.01)
HIGH (amount>=5000 rule fires despite self-contaminated avg)
MEDIUM (avg=$291.50 — 5 seed txns diluted by self-inclusion, ratio=5.1)
MEDIUM (avg=$291.67 self-contaminated, ratio=5.1 — should be HIGH at 30x)

bugs.title: Transaction inserted before average lookup — self-contamination of baseline
bugs.root_cause: main.py:34 INSERTs the transaction into SQLite, then agent.py:74 calls get_user_average which queries the same table — including the just-inserted row. A new user's first transaction always has avg=amount, ratio=1.0, bypassing all ratio-based detection.
bugs.impact: Ratio-based HIGH/MEDIUM detection is completely defeated for first-time users. Case 7b ($3000 on new user, 30x baseline) returned LOW instead of HIGH. Case 3 ($4999.99 new user) returned LOW instead of MEDIUM/HIGH.
bugs.fix: Call get_user_average BEFORE the INSERT in main.py, or exclude the current transaction ID from the avg subquery in database.py.

bugs.title: Duplicate transaction ID causes unhandled 500
cases_affected: observed during re-run with reused IDs
bugs.root_cause: The INSERT in main.py has no error handling for the UNIQUE PRIMARY KEY constraint on transactions.id. SQLite raises IntegrityError which propagates as a 500.
bugs.impact: Any client retrying a request with the same ID (idempotency pattern) gets a 500 instead of 409 or the original result.
bugs.fix: Wrap the INSERT in try/except IntegrityError and return 409 Conflict, or use INSERT OR IGNORE with a subsequent SELECT.

bugs.title: Negative amounts accepted and silently classified LOW
bugs.root_cause: Pydantic model has no ge=0 constraint on amount. A negative amount makes ratio negative (avg<0 -> baseline=100, ratio<0), which falls through all thresholds to LOW. The heuristic explanation reads 'Amount $-100.00 is within normal range'.
bugs.impact: Fraudulent negative-amount transactions (chargebacks, refund fraud) are never flagged. The explanation text is nonsensical.
bugs.fix: Add ge=0 or gt=0 constraint to the amount field in models.py: amount: float = Field(..., ge=0)

bugs.title: No field length limits — 5000-char location stored without truncation
bugs.root_cause: No max_length constraints on string fields in models.py. SQLite TEXT has no limit, but large inputs bloat the DB, inflate LLM prompts (cost/latency), and could DoS the DeepSeek API.
bugs.fix: Add max_length constraints in models.py, e.g. location: str = Field(..., max_length=255)

bugs.title: Empty and non-date timestamps accepted silently
bugs.root_cause: timestamp is typed as str with no datetime validation. Empty string '' and 'not-a-date' are stored verbatim. The ORDER BY timestamp DESC sort in list_transactions will produce undefined ordering for malformed timestamps.
bugs.fix: Type timestamp as datetime in models.py, or add a validator. FastAPI/Pydantic v2 will then reject malformed inputs with 422.

bugs.title: Already-frozen users are re-frozen on subsequent HIGH transactions
bugs.root_cause: freeze_user() in database.py uses INSERT OR IGNORE + UPDATE unconditionally. There is no check in agent.py or main.py for whether an account is already frozen before processing or before calling freeze_user again.
bugs.fix: Add a pre-analysis check: if user already frozen, still classify but skip the freeze_user call and set action_taken='already_frozen'.

brief_for_next: Phase 4 (security-prober) should prioritize: (1) SQL injection via id/user_id/merchant/location fields — no parameterization audit done yet; (2) the self-contamination bug (BUG-1) means any ratio-based fraud rule is completely bypassable by a new user — test whether an attacker can exploit this to launder HIGH-risk transactions; (3) no auth on any endpoint; (4) CORS wildcard. The heuristic explanations seen in DeepSeek mode suggest the LLM may be pattern-matching the heuristic format, making it hard to distinguish LLM vs fallback mode from output alone — the security-prober should verify prompt injection resistance.
